message:
feat(hostcall): add AMAC interleaved batch executor, regime-shift detector, and CUSUM/BOCPD budget controller augmentation

Introduce three interconnected adaptive dispatch mechanisms that dynamically
optimize hostcall throughput based on observed workload characteristics.

New module: src/hostcall_amac.rs (1152 lines)
- AmacBatchExecutor: AMAC (Asynchronous Memory Access Chaining) interleaved
  batch executor that overlaps computation of one request with memory fetches
  of another, improving throughput when working set exceeds LLC
- AmacBatchExecutorConfig: configurable min_batch_size, max_interleave_width,
  stall_threshold_ns, and EMA parameters for adaptive toggling
- Stall-aware mode switching: dynamically toggles between batched-interleaved
  and sequential dispatch based on observed per-call timing telemetry as proxy
  for LLC miss rates and stall cycles
- Registered as pub mod hostcall_amac in lib.rs

New in src/extension_dispatcher.rs (520 lines added):
- RegimeShiftDetector: adaptive regime detector for hostcall workload shifts
  using composite scoring of queue depth, service time, opcode entropy, and
  LLC miss rate, with dual CUSUM/BOCPD change-point detection
- RegimeAdaptationMode enum: SequentialFastPath vs InterleavedBatching
- Confirmation streak + cooldown to prevent mode thrashing
- Fallback heuristic when statistical detection is unreliable
- ExtensionDispatcher gains regime_detector field (RefCell)

Extended in src/extensions.rs (~1350 lines changed):
- RegimeShiftConfig: per-tier CUSUM k/h and BOCPD lambda/threshold parameters
  (Strict more sensitive, Throughput more conservative)
- CusumState: CUSUM detector with online Welford baseline estimation, dual
  cumsum_high/cumsum_low for bidirectional rate change detection, alarm with
  auto-reset
- BocpdState: Bayesian Online Change Point Detection with run-length
  distribution, Gaussian observation model, online mean/variance, and pruning
  at configurable max_run_length horizon
- ExtensionBudgetControllerConfig gains regime_shift field wired into all
  three tier presets
- Budget controller's overload signal path now optionally triggers early
  fallback when CUSUM+BOCPD detect statistically significant regime shifts
- Integration with AmacBatchExecutor and HostcallSuperinstructionCompiler
  via new imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
